notes.note: Litterae commendaticiae: formal letters of recommendation, a routine instrument of Roman patronage by which a man asked a powerful friend to favour a third party. Cicero notes he had meant to send Antonius only these, to keep up appearances despite their cooled friendship.

T. Pomponius Atticus, Cicero's lifelong intimate, here travelling to Antonius (then governing Macedonia) on business of his own. The whole letter is built around commending Atticus's affairs to Antonius.

notes.anchor: that very word which men say is wont to be falsely turned against me
notes.note: The verb comperisse (``I have ascertained''), which Cicero used of his evidence against the Catilinarian conspirators and which his enemies mockingly threw back at him as a sign of overreach. He pointedly avoids it here even while hinting at Antonius's disloyalty.
